name: Darnell Washington
bio: Darnell is the unofficial mayor of Monday Nite Football. A lifelong KC native, he grew up watching games at Arrowhead with his dad and has carried that tradition forward with the MNF crew.
communities: Chiefs Kingdom, Westport Neighborhood Association, KC BBQ Society
fun_fact: Can recite every Chiefs Super Bowl roster from memory

name: Marcus Jennings
bio: Marcus brought the competitive edge to MNF. A former college wide receiver at UMKC, he channels his athletic background into fierce fantasy football strategy and even fiercer trash talk.
communities: UMKC Alumni Network, Brookside Merchants Association, KC Parks & Rec
fun_fact: Still holds the MNF record for most trash talk per quarter

name: Kevin Okafor
bio: Kevin is the culture connector of the group. As a local artist and muralist, he's woven MNF into the creative fabric of Crossroads, hosting watch parties at his studio every season.
communities: Crossroads Arts District Collective, KC Art Institute, First Fridays KC
fun_fact: Has painted two murals inspired by MNF game nights

name: Raymond Torres
bio: Ray brings the hospitality. A restaurateur with two spots on the Plaza, he ensures the MNF crew eats well every Monday — his green chile nachos are legendary among members.
communities: KC Restaurant Association, Plaza Merchants, Hispanic Chamber of Commerce KC
fun_fact: Once catered a watch party for 60 people out of his car trunk

name: DeShawn Williams
bio: DeShawn grew up on Troost and is passionate about the neighborhood's renaissance. He uses MNF as a platform to connect local businesses and uplift his community.
communities: Troost Community Alliance, KC Urban Youth Foundation, Black Chamber of Commerce KC
fun_fact: Organized the first MNF charity game to benefit Troost rec centers

name: James Calloway
bio: James is the stats guy. A data analyst by trade, he built a custom MNF performance tracker that has become the official record book of the group — down to yards per carry per snack break.
communities: Waldo Area Business Association, KC Data Science Meetup, Chiefs Analytics Community
fun_fact: His spreadsheet has over 10,000 rows of MNF game data

name: Terrence Bell
bio: Terrence is the heartbeat of the group. A pastor and community organizer, he keeps MNF grounded in friendship and purpose, often turning game nights into impromptu community conversations.
communities: Midtown KC Community Board, KC Interfaith Council, Historic Vine District Advocates
fun_fact: Once paused a playoff game to mediate a neighborhood dispute — and won both

name: Andre Mitchell
bio: Andre is the newest member to find his footing but quickest to make his mark. A tech entrepreneur building KC's startup scene, he introduced the MNF group chat and single-handedly upgraded the streaming setup.
communities: KC Startup Village, River Market Community Association, Digital Sandbox KC
fun_fact: Pitched a MNF mobile app on Shark Tank (spoiler: didn't get a deal)
